name: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate

domains.name: Domain 1: Data Preparation for Machine Learning
Domain 3: Deployment and Orchestration of ML Workflows
Domain 4: ML Solution Monitoring, Maintenance, and Security

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to visually prepare tabular data for ML without code?
questions.explanation: Data Wrangler imports, transforms, and exports ML data.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to run ETL on large datasets in a data lake?
questions.explanation: Glue provides serverless Spark ETL.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to label images for object detection?
questions.explanation: Ground Truth manages human labeling workflows.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to detect schema and statistics on S3 data?

options.text: AWS Glue Data Catalog and crawlers

questions.explanation: The Data Catalog stores table metadata.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to handle class imbalance in training data?

options.text: Oversampling, weights, or SMOTE techniques in SageMaker

questions.explanation: Imbalanced data requires sampling or weighted loss.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to split data into train/validation/test without leakage?

options.text: SageMaker Processing or sklearn split with stratification

questions.explanation: Proper splits prevent optimistic metrics.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to store feature values for online inference?
questions.explanation: Feature Store serves consistent features in training and inference.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to anonymize PII before model training?
questions.explanation: Remove or tokenize sensitive attributes early.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to train models with managed infrastructure and spot savings?
questions.explanation: Training jobs scale on ML instances with spot support.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to tune hyperparameters automatically?
questions.explanation: Automatic Model Tuning searches hyperparameter space.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to track experiments and parameters?
questions.explanation: Experiment tracking compares runs reproducibly.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to use built-in algorithms for common ML tasks?
questions.explanation: Built-ins include XGBoost, Linear Learner, and more.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to bring custom Docker container for training?

options.text: Arbitrary root on public hub only

questions.explanation: Custom containers package proprietary dependencies.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to distributed training across multiple GPUs?
questions.explanation: Distributed libraries scale deep learning jobs.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to version datasets and models together?

options.text: SageMaker Model Registry with dataset snapshots in S3

questions.explanation: Versioning supports rollback and audit.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to debug training with real-time metrics?
questions.explanation: Debugger detects issues like vanishing gradients.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to deploy real-time inference behind an endpoint?
questions.explanation: Endpoints serve low-latency predictions.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to run asynchronous inference for large payloads?
questions.explanation: Async inference queues requests and returns results later.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to deploy multiple models behind one endpoint?

options.text: One endpoint per tiny model always costly

questions.explanation: Multi-model endpoints share infrastructure.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to orchestrate ML CI/CD steps?
questions.explanation: Pipelines define reproducible ML workflows.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to use infrastructure as code for SageMaker resources?
questions.explanation: IaC codifies ML infrastructure.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to A/B test model variants in production?

options.text: SageMaker production variants with traffic weights

questions.explanation: Traffic splitting compares model performance safely.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to containerize inference for Kubernetes?

options.text: SageMaker Inference Recommender or EKS hosting

questions.explanation: EKS can host containers built for SageMaker.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to schedule batch scoring nightly?

options.text: SageMaker Batch Transform or scheduled pipelines

questions.explanation: Batch Transform efficiently scores large datasets.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to detect data drift in production features?
questions.explanation: Model Monitor compares live data to baselines.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to explain individual predictions to stakeholders?
questions.explanation: Clarify provides SHAP and other explainability.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to encrypt training volumes and model artifacts?

options.text: KMS encryption on EBS and S3 for SageMaker

questions.explanation: Encryption protects ML intellectual property.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to restrict notebook internet access for compliance?
questions.explanation: VPC-only Studio limits data exfiltration.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to capture inference payloads for audit (where allowed)?
questions.explanation: Data capture records inputs/outputs per policy.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to automatically roll back bad deployments?

options.text: CloudWatch alarms + CodeDeploy or pipeline approvals

questions.explanation: Automated rollback limits incident blast radius.

questions.explanation: Inspector scans images in ECR.

questions.explanation: Logging supports security investigations.

questions.text: Which TWO practices align with the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Design for failure and automate recovery
Use single-AZ for all production data stores

questions.explanation: Reliability and operational excellence emphasize automation, testing, and learning from failure.

questions.text: Which are AWS services used for encryption or key management? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: KMS manages keys; Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets.

questions.text: Which TWO official AWS resources help you prepare for AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Use official AWS exam guides, Skill Builder, and AWS documentation—not real exam content.

questions.text: According to AWS best practices documented in 'Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler', what should you do FIRST when addressing Data Preparation for Machine Learning objectives?

options.text: Review the official AWS documentation and align designs to the exam guide domains
Disable security logging to reduce noise
Use root user credentials for daily operations

questions.explanation: Official guides such as Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler describe domain objectives; practice designs should follow AWS documented best practices.
